name: Courtbouillon of Redfish
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the fish and remove the head and tail. Cut across the backbone into slices that are 2 to 3 inches wide.
2. In a Dutch oven, heat the butter over medium heat. Once melted, stir in the flour until smooth, cooking for 10 minutes until brown, stirring constantly.
3. Reduce the heat to low. Stir in the onion, bell pepper, and celery.
4. Cook the vegetables, stirring often, until they are soft.
5. Add the tomatoes (with their liquid), cook for 5 minutes, then stir in the herbs, seasonings, hot pepper sauce, Worcestershire sauce, parsley, garlic, bay leaves, marjoram, oregano, basil, and thyme.
6. Slowly stir in the reserved tomato liquid and water until combined. Simmer for 5 minutes.
7. Add the fish slices to a platter and remove any bones. Return the fish slices to the "courtbouillon."
8. Add white wine to the pot. Cover and bring to a boil over high heat. Serve immediately in soup plates with hot cooked rice.
